risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: The project faces a High political and governance risk rating that persists from approval through current implementation. This risk category is rated High in the Systematic Operations Risk-Rating Tool, indicating significant political instability or governance fragility that could impede project execution in Karachi.

The project is operating under High macroeconomic risk conditions that were present at approval and remain at High level. The document notes that the KWSC Operating Cost Coverage Ratio declined due to 'exogenous factors including increase in electricity tariff and government mandated increase in employee salaries,' indicating macroeconomic pressures affecting project financial sustainability.

risk_title: Deteriorating Utility Financial Performance Due to External Economic Pressures
risk_summary: The KWSC Operating Cost Coverage Ratio has declined from 76% (baseline) to 61% (current), moving away from the target of 130%. This decline is attributed to government-mandated salary increases and electricity tariff increases, indicating external economic pressures constraining the utility's financial capacity to implement the project.
evidence_quote: While revenues improved, OCCR declined due in part to exogenous factors including increase in electricity tariff and government mandated increase in employee salaries
